QF.id: VIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_59

question.text: If the spot price of an index is 20,000, risk-free rate is 6%, dividend yield is 2%, and time to expiry is 30 days, what is the theoretical futures price?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Step-by-step calculation: F = S x (1 + r - q)^(T/365) F = 20,000 x (1 + 0.06 - 0.02)^(30/365) F = 20,000 x (1.04)^(0.0822) F = 20,000 x 1.003226 F = Rs. 20,065.42 (approximately)
Note: For short durations, the premium is small.
**The Trap:** Rs. 20,800 uses full-year carry (20000 x 1.04). For just 30 days, the carry is much smaller. Rs. 20,400 uses simple annual calculation. Always apply the time fraction.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Short-duration (near-month) futures pricing is commonly tested in Series VIII.
**Key Takeaway:** For 30-day contracts, the cost of carry premium is very small. F = 20,000 x (1.04)^(30/365) = Rs. 20,065.
